title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI is changing software development practices and material interaction. He explores the tension between AI abstraction and maintaining transparency, craftsmanship, and connection to the building process itself.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Cursor's Ryo Lu examines how AI is reshaping software development practices and the way developers interact with their material. The central theme is the tension between AI abstraction and the values of transparency, craftsmanship, and a direct connection to the act of building. The talk matters because higher-level AI assistance can distance engineers from the underlying work, and Lu frames staying "closer to the material" as a deliberate counterweight.
